title: Jayson Tatum is ‘not thrilled’ about first trip to Madison Square Garden since injury
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum is set to return to Madison Square Garden 11 months after rupturing his Achilles tendon there.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's hard work and recovery, noting his readiness to perform well.
The Celtics are likely to enter the playoffs as the No. 2 seed, with a potential playoff rematch against the Knicks.

title: 5 Bruins thoughts on a winless road trip, James Hagens, and playoff matchups
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins went winless on their recent four-game road trip, ending with a 6-5 overtime loss to the Hurricanes.
Despite recent struggles, the Bruins' playoff odds remain at 99 percent, with potential clinching scenarios involving upcoming games.
Morgan Geekie ended a 17-game goalless streak with a hat trick against Carolina, offering hope for the playoff push.

title: When she was 11, Vanna Howard was in a refugee camp. Now she’s the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Vanna Howard is the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S., representing the 1st Middlesex District.
Howard's priorities include addressing the housing crisis, improving public education, and expanding health care access.
Howard emigrated from Cambodia after living in a Thai refugee camp, influenced by her experiences to support immigrant communities.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 8)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A condo unit at 1 Dalton St. in Boston sold for $14 million, the highest in the area.
A one-family ranch at 6 Gordon Road in Arlington sold for $1,725,000.
A three-family flat at 34 Hillberg Ave. in Brockton sold for $750,000.

title: What businesses in Boston are worth waiting in line for?
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brandy Melville on Newbury Street in Boston sees consistent queues due to its exclusive 'one size fits all' clothing offer.
Blank Street's Boylston Street coffee shop employs line security to manage frequent long waits for their popular products.
Brick Street Bagels in South Boston draws crowds with its online fame, sparking debates over its worth and pricing.

title: Mass. woman pleads guilty to performing medical spa injections using counterfeit products
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli, 40, pleaded guilty to using counterfeit products for cosmetic injections at her two Massachusetts spas.
Fadanelli admitted to importing fake Botox and fillers from China and Brazil, charging over $1 million for procedures.
She faces up to 20 years in prison and a $250,000 fine for importing and selling counterfeit goods.

title: Man fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University campus identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jacob Graves, 29, was shot and killed by Boston police on Hemenway Street near Northeastern University during a mental health crisis.
Multiple officers and emergency responders were injured after Graves attacked them with a 'sword-like weapon', prompting police to open fire.
The shooting is under investigation; it's part of a series of violent incidents near Northeastern's campus in recent weeks.

title: Celtics’ Brad Stevens addresses ‘tiresome’ college basketball rumors
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics' Brad Stevens dismisses rumors of leaving for college basketball, stating no plans to depart Boston.
UNC considered Stevens for their coaching role before hiring Mike Malone, with Stevens declining the opportunity.
Stevens, Celtics' president since 2021, remains focused on maintaining team competitiveness in the NBA playoffs.

title: Man gets life sentence in 2024 killing of Boston father of 2
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Widmaer Lapaix sentenced to life with parole possible after 15 years for the 2024 murder of Maceo Martin.
Maceo Martin was shot by Lapaix during a dispute outside a Cambridge apartment building on October 8, 2024.
Lapaix was convicted of second-degree murder after a nine-day trial, as confirmed by Suffolk County District Attorney.

title: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if trial is split into 2 parts, defense says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's defense seeks a two-part trial where she admits to killing her children, focusing on her mental state.
Judge William Sullivan previously denied splitting Clancy's trial, stating guilt and criminal responsibility issues are intertwined.
Clancy is accused of strangling her three children in Duxbury in 2023 and showing extreme mental distress symptoms prior.
